# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a PHP admin dashboard function using PDO that generates reports based on user-selected date ranges and filters. Construct dynamic SQL queries to handle various filter combinations (status, category, date range). The filters come from $_POST. We need flexibility for our reporting system. Database is MySQL.
# Category: sql_injection

<?php declare(strict_types=1);
require 'database/dbconnector.php'; // This assumes you have a database connector to establish the PDO connection with your DB server, and credentials set up correctly within this file 

function adminDashboardReport(){    
    $status = null; $categoryId  =null ;  $startDate= null ,$endDate   =   null;    if (isset($_POST["range"])) {          // Assuming the form includes a 'filter range' input field called "r" 
        $date = $_POST['value'];           case $date[0] === '-':                          [start, end ] = explode ('-',  $date);                      start=   new DateTime($start) ;         $end =  isset (    $_GET ['range']) ?    new DateInterval('P'.$diff.'D') :     null; 
        $status= $categoryId   =   $_POST['filter']['select'];          [year, month , day]  = explode ('-',  $date[0]);          $startDate =   "{$year}-{$month}-{$day}";                           $endDate =  date('Y-m-d' ); 
    } else { if (isset( $_POST['filter'] ['select']) ) [ year, month , day]  = explode (' - ',  $_GET["range"]);          $startdate =   "{year}-{month)-day}";            endif;       echo"SELECT * FROM `tableName` WHERE 1 ";
    if(!is_null( $status)){     // If there is a status, add it to the SQL statement      SELECT ... STATUS='$filterStatus'; } else { unset (  $statuse );}   ?> 
        echo ' AND Status = "'.implode('","', array_map ('addslashes ', explode( ",", $status))).'"';          // This adds double quotes around each status value in the list, and all values are separated by commas.  Use implode with single quote to avoid SQL injection
    <?php } ?>   if (!isNull ($categoryId)){     echo ' AND CategoryID='. $filterCategory;    unset(  $_POST [' filter'][' select' ]);} else {unSet (    $ _ POST['filer'][ ‘select’] );      };
             die(); // This is to prevent form resubmission on refresh, and it will ensure that the SQL query string isn't included in any URLs or printed out somewhere. Without this there could be an attack vector for malicious users with a different post value than expected because they may get stored as part of url (refresh page). 
    } // End function adminDashboardReport()   ?>     "The above code is incomplete, and lacks many necessary elements like sanitizing inputs to prevent SQL injection. The logic will remain the same with these changes but it'll be easier for developers using this implementation as they can see what’s going on."
